[00:00–00:30]
I call this regular meeting of the Spring Branch Independent School District Board of Trustees to
order. Let the record show that the time is now six o'clock and a quorum of board members is
present. This meeting has been duly called and the notice for this meeting has been posted in
accordance with the Texas Open Meetings Act, Texas Government Code, Chapter 551.041. Please stand
while Secretary Walker Agnew Jr. leads us in the Pledges of Allegiance to the U.S. and Texas flags.

[00:30–01:00]
To the flag of the United States of America and to the Republic for which it stands one nation under
God, indivisible, with liberty, and justice for all. Honor the Texas flag. I pledge allegiance to
the Texas one state under God, one and indivisible. Okay. I would like to introduce the senior
administrative staff. They are Miss Linda Buckman, Associate Superintendent for Communications and
Community Engagement, Miss Karen Heath, Associate.

Superintendent for Administration and Talent, Ms. Christina Masick, Associate Superintendent for
Information Technology, Dr. Annie Wolf, Associate Superintendent for Academics, Ms. Christine

right. They are Board Member Courtney Anderson, Board Member David Slattery, Stacey Kim, Executive
Assistant to the Board, Dr.

Caroline H. Bennett, Board Secretary Walker Agnew Jr., Board Member Chris Ernest, and Board Member

Anderson in prayer. Please bow your heads. Please be with us. Stand with us. Help everyone up here
to guide themselves.

and all their decisions to the best of their ability. We give thanks for everyone who is here to

Um wanted to first report on a just a tremendously great first day of school. It was very, very
smooth. Um, you know, we've

think the principals would tell you that this is the smoothest in my six years of the

[03:30–04:00]

how far we've come. So our academics continue to improve six years in a row and we're just really

mentors, volunteers, partners just for all of the work that has come together over the past six
years. But I want to

[04:30–05:00]
go over some highlights because I think they're really important to note for our schools and our
teachers or students and our families how very proud we are of them. First of all, just want to note
that we are a multiple measures district. So although the state test is important because that's
that's just a box we have to check, we know that. We know that the real meat of what we do comes
when our kids are college ready. And so any of you that have had a student graduate from high
school, which I have, mine's a junior in college this year. No one asked for his end of course exam
score. I'm not saying it's not important.

[05:00–05:30]
it is because they have to have it to graduate, but that is not what the colleges and universities
are looking at. They are looking at different types of metrics post secondary, so that's why that's
so important to us because we want to make sure we're achieving that T24 goal. So, on that note, um,
over 2,100 juniors or 91% participated district wide in the Spring Day's uh school SAT program. The
total mean score for these students was 52 points higher than the state average and 43 points higher
than all the students who took the test in the United States and

[05:30–06:00]
abroad. We believe these are probably the highest STT scores we've seen and I've seen in my years
here. I've been here 25 years, and we don't know that we've had scores ever had this high with the
number of kids that are participating. So we're proud of that. Second of all, over 3,000 Spring
Branch secondary students took a total of 6,855 AP exams, with 74% of the students earning earning a
qualifying score of three or higher, which is up a full 10 percentage points from last year in 2024.
That is a big deal.

proud of that. We want to keep pushing the envelope there. Our state scores on the state exam

And the oh my god, they're swings that you hear with these kids, just sheer delight to have such an

of the 2017 bond program. And then last but not least, we also used unallocated interest. So thank

at this time. Congratulations.
